topic: Should universities be free for all students?

systemArguments.text: Free higher education would eliminate student debt and make college accessible to everyone regardless of economic background
Society benefits when more people have advanced degrees and specialized skills
Education should be based on merit and ability, not on family wealth or financial resources
Public investment in higher education drives innovation and economic competitiveness
Free universities would create a more skilled workforce and reduce inequality
Many successful countries already provide free or low-cost higher education

goldenArguments.text: Education is a fundamental human right that should not depend on wealth
Equal opportunity requires removing financial barriers to higher education
A just society invests in the intellectual development of all its citizens
Merit and ability should determine university admission, not financial capacity
Free higher education increases economic mobility and reduces inequality
Public investment in universities drives innovation and economic growth
Universal access to higher education creates a more skilled workforce
Free tuition eliminates the burden of student debt on young adults
Society benefits when talent is developed regardless of economic background

humanRationale: System arguments are well-formed and capture key policy concerns, but they combine multiple concepts (accessibility + debt relief) and focus more on practical outcomes rather than underlying values. The golden set includes both broad value claims (education as right, equal opportunity, justice) and specific policy claims (economic mobility, workforce development). System partially captures the policy dimension but misses the deeper value-based arguments that motivate positions on this issue.
